skill.name: amazon-buy-box-monitor-api-skill
skill.description: This skill helps users extract basic product details other sellers prices and seller ratings from Amazon via ASIN automatically using the BrowserAct API. Agent should proactively apply this skill when users express needs like query Amazon buy box information, monitor Amazon product prices, extract Amazon product details by ASIN, check other sellers prices on Amazon, get Amazon seller ratings and feedback count, monitor buy box ownership for a specific ASIN, track Amazon fulfillment methods for competitors, compare Amazon product prices across different sellers, retrieve Amazon buy box availability status, analyze Amazon seller profile details.
skill.summary: Monitor Amazon buy box ownership, competitor prices, and seller ratings via ASIN using BrowserAct API
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Security audit completed. All static findings are legitimate API integration patterns. Network access is restricted to the BrowserAct API endpoint only. API key storage uses environment variables (standard secure practice). No command injection vulnerabilities exist. The external_commands detection in SKILL.md are false positives from documentation markdown code blocks.

high_findings.title: API Key Environment Variable Access
high_findings.description: Script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from environment variables for authentication. This is legitimate credential management pattern.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard environment variable pattern for API key management - not a security vulnerability but an expected operational requirement

low_findings.title: Hardcoded API Endpoint URLs
low_findings.description: API endpoint URLs are hardcoded in the script. These point to the legitimate BrowserAct API service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Hardcoded URLs are for the BrowserAct API service - legitimate API integration, not suspicious network activity

low_findings.title: External Commands in Documentation
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains bash command examples for script invocation - these are documentation, not actual command execution
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Markdown code blocks showing how to invoke the script are documentation patterns, not command injection vectors

content.user_title: Monitor Amazon Buy Box, Prices, and Seller Ratings by ASIN
content.value_statement: Track Amazon buy box ownership, competitor pricing, and seller ratings without web scraping. Uses BrowserAct API to extract structured data from any Amazon ASIN.
actual_capabilities: Extract buy box owner and price for any Amazon ASIN
Retrieve competing seller prices and fulfillment methods (FBA/FBM)
Get seller ratings and feedback counts
Check product availability and stock status
Monitor multiple Amazon marketplaces (US, UK, etc.)
Access structured data without web scraping or CAPTCHA handling
limitations: Requires BrowserAct API key for operation
Marketplace URLs must match Amazon domain structure
Data extraction speed depends on BrowserAct API response times

use_cases.title: E-commerce Seller Price Intelligence
use_cases.description: Track competitor prices for your product catalog. Identify when rivals undercut your buy box position or adjust pricing strategies.

use_cases.title: Product Research and Sourcing
use_cases.description: Evaluate potential products by checking buy box prices, seller ratings, and fulfillment competition before adding to your inventory.
use_cases.target_user: Product researchers and private label sellers

use_cases.title: Buy Box Ownership Monitoring
use_cases.description: Set up alerts when buy box ownership changes for specific ASINs. Monitor your own position against competitors.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Buy Box Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Amazon Buy Box Monitor skill to check the buy box owner and price for ASIN B005O2ZU68 on amazon.com

prompt_templates.title: Full Product Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Get a complete analysis of ASIN B005O2ZU68 including buy box price, competing sellers, fulfillment methods, and seller ratings
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants comprehensive product competitive data

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Marketplace Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare buy box data for ASIN B005O2ZU68 across amazon.com, amazon.co.uk, and amazon.de
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants international market comparison

prompt_templates.title: Seller Profile Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the current buy box owner for ASIN B005O2ZU68 and get their seller rating and feedback count
prompt_templates.scenario: When evaluating a specific seller's reputation

output_examples.input: Check the buy box status for ASIN B005O2ZU68 on amazon.com
output_examples.output:
Buy Box Owner: Amazon.com
Buy Box Price: $24.99
Currency: USD
Fulfillment: FBA
Availability: In Stock
Other Sellers: 3 competing sellers with prices ranging from $22.50 to $29.99

output_examples.input: Get competitor pricing data for ASIN B005O2ZU68
output_examples.output:
Top 3 Sellers:
1. Amazon.com - $24.99 (FBA) - Rating: 4.8/5 - 12,500 feedbacks
2. TechDeals Inc - $22.50 + $5.99 shipping (FBM) - Rating: 4.2/5 - 890 feedbacks
3. QuickShip Store - $23.99 + $2.99 shipping (FBM) - Rating: 4.5/5 - 340 feedbacks

best_practices: Store your BrowserAct API key securely in environment variables, never hardcode it
Use the default amazon.com marketplace unless targeting a specific international market
Check ASIN format before querying - Amazon ASINs are 10 alphanumeric characters
anti_patterns: Do not pass unvalidated ASINs to the script - verify format matches Amazon ASIN standards
Avoid rapid repeated queries for the same ASIN - implement rate limiting in your workflow
Do not share your BrowserAct API key across multiple users or systems

faq.question: What is an Amazon ASIN?
faq.answer: ASIN stands for Amazon Standard Identification Number. It is a 10-character unique identifier assigned to products on Amazon.

faq.question: What marketplaces are supported?
faq.answer: Any Amazon marketplace URL is supported including amazon.com, amazon.co.uk, amazon.de, amazon.fr, and other regional sites.

faq.question: Do I need a BrowserAct account?
faq.answer: Yes, you need a BrowserAct API key to use this skill. Get one from browseract.com/reception/integrations.

faq.question: How long does a query take?
faq.answer: Queries typically complete within a few minutes depending on BrowserAct API response times. The script polls for completion.

faq.question: What data is returned?
faq.answer: Returns buy box owner, price, fulfillment method, availability, competing sellers with prices, and seller ratings.

faq.question: Is web scraping required?
faq.answer: No, this skill uses the BrowserAct API which handles browser automation and CAPTCHA internally.
